description: Christopher Pincher was appointed Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) on 8 February 2022. He was previously Minister of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on 19 September 2021. He was first elected Conservative MP for Tamworth in May 2010. Christopher was previously Minister of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 13 February 2020 to 19 September 2021, Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office from 25 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Deputy Chief Whip, Treasurer of HM Household from 9 January 2018 to 25 July 2019. He was also Comptroller of HM Household (Government Whip) from June 2017 to November 2017 and was an Assistant Government Whip from 17 July 2016 until 15 June 2017.

details.body:
Christopher Pincher was appointed Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) on 8 February 2022.

He was previously Minister of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on 19 September 2021. He was first elected Conservative MP for Tamworth in May 2010.

Christopher was previously Minister of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 13 February 2020 to 19 September 2021, Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office from 25 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Deputy Chief Whip, Treasurer of HM Household from 9 January 2018 to 25 July 2019.

He was also Comptroller of HM Household (Government Whip) from June 2017 to November 2017 and was an Assistant Government Whip from 17 July 2016 until 15 June 2017.

Christopher Pincher was appointed Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) on 8 February 2022.

He was previously Minister of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on 19 September 2021. He was first elected Conservative MP for Tamworth in May 2010.

Christopher was previously Minister of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 13 February 2020 to 19 September 2021, Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office from 25 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Deputy Chief Whip, Treasurer of HM Household from 9 January 2018 to 25 July 2019.

He was also Comptroller of HM Household (Government Whip) from June 2017 to November 2017 and was an Assistant Government Whip from 17 July 2016 until 15 June 2017.

Whips are MPs or Lords appointed by each party to help organise their party’s contribution to parliamentary business. They are responsible for making sure the maximum number of their party members vote, and vote the way their party wants.

role.title: Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Christopher Pincher MP - Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip)

details.body:
Christopher Pincher was appointed Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) on 8 February 2022.

He was previously Minister of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on 19 September 2021. He was first elected Conservative MP for Tamworth in May 2010.

Christopher was previously Minister of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 13 February 2020 to 19 September 2021, Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office from 25 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Deputy Chief Whip, Treasurer of HM Household from 9 January 2018 to 25 July 2019.

He was also Comptroller of HM Household (Government Whip) from June 2017 to November 2017 and was an Assistant Government Whip from 17 July 2016 until 15 June 2017.

• Planning casework
  • Tackling leasehold and freehold abuses
  • Housing Supply
  • Planning reform
  • Design
  • Homeownership
  • Homebuying and Selling
  • Voluntary Right to Buy
  • Affordable Housing Programme
  • Legislation: Levelling Up & Regeneration Bill (together with Levelling Up Minister)

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Christopher Pincher MP - Minister of State (Minister for Housing)

details.body:
Christopher Pincher was appointed Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) on 8 February 2022.

He was previously Minister of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on 19 September 2021. He was first elected Conservative MP for Tamworth in May 2010.

Christopher was previously Minister of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 13 February 2020 to 19 September 2021, Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office from 25 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Deputy Chief Whip, Treasurer of HM Household from 9 January 2018 to 25 July 2019.

He was also Comptroller of HM Household (Government Whip) from June 2017 to November 2017 and was an Assistant Government Whip from 17 July 2016 until 15 June 2017.

Responsibilities include:

Housing policy: including

  • Home ownership
  • Housing strategy
  • Housing delivery
  • Homes England Stewardship
  • Affordable Housing Programme
  • Design and Building Better
  • Planning reform
  • Commons Minister on Building Safety
  • Winter preparedness (Transition and Covid, including Deputy at XO and Covid-O)
  • Project Speed
  • Ox-Cam
  • Homebuying and selling
  • Voluntary Right to Buy
  • Private Rented Sector
  • Planning casework

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (2018 to 2021)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Christopher Pincher MP - Minister of State (Minister for Housing)

details.body:
Christopher Pincher was appointed Treasurer of HM Household (Deputy Chief Whip) on 8 February 2022.

He was previously Minister of State at the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on 19 September 2021. He was first elected Conservative MP for Tamworth in May 2010.

Christopher was previously Minister of State at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government from 13 February 2020 to 19 September 2021, Minister of State at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office from 25 July 2019 to 13 February 2020 and Deputy Chief Whip, Treasurer of HM Household from 9 January 2018 to 25 July 2019.

He was also Comptroller of HM Household (Government Whip) from June 2017 to November 2017 and was an Assistant Government Whip from 17 July 2016 until 15 June 2017.

Responsibilities include:

  • the Americas, including the Falklands
  • Europe, including Gibraltar, Sovereign Base Areas and third country agreements
  • treaty policy and practice
  • deputy to the Foreign Secretary for EU Exit Cabinet Committees
  • Eastern Europe and Central Asia
  • Euro-Atlantic security policy
  • OSCE, Council of Europe and sanctions
  • relations with Parliament

role.title: Minister of State (Minister for Europe and the Americas)
